root_motivation: To compare ethical philosophies deductively, they must reason over the same shared vocabulary. No framework-neutral moral TBox currently exists that a consequentialist, deontologist, and virtue ethicist can all range over. Without it, 'compare two philosophies' is not even expressible. This PRD ships the shared vocabulary — a BFO-grounded, framework-neutral moral-domain TBox compiled to OWL 2 DL — as the foundation every subsequent doxa framework module will build on.
user_persona: Joe Yen, running doxa on a Linux laptop as part of the wintermute ecosystem. Builds moral reasoning infrastructure for AI systems; needs the shared vocabulary before any framework module can be written.

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes in ~/wintermute/doxa.
doxa build-core --out /tmp/core.owl exits 0 and writes a non-empty OWL file (when ousia-forge is on $PATH; skip with logged note if absent).
ousia-forge check --spec spec-core/ (or doxa check-core) reports the spec valid.
The built core ontology contains ≥12 moral-domain classes and ≥8 object properties.
RightAction, WrongAction, PermissibleAction are declared as subclasses of Action but carry no equivalence/definition axiom in core.
Every core class has a BFO parent IRI and a non-empty definition annotation.
doxa check-core exits non-zero on a deliberately malformed spec fixture.

scope: A Rust CLI binary `doxa` with `build-core` and `check-core` subcommands
A spec-core/ TOML spec in ousia-forge format declaring ≥12 framework-neutral moral-domain classes and ≥8 object properties
All core classes grounded to BFO parents with non-empty definitions
RightAction/WrongAction/PermissibleAction declared as subclasses of Action, left undefined in core
non_goals: No framework modules (consequentialist, deontological, virtue-theoretic) — those are the next PRD
No modification of the ousia-forge binary itself

additional.ousia_forge_integration: shells out to ousia-forge binary; does not link it as a library

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a framework-neutral moral TBox?
five_whys_trace.a: To make ethical frameworks commensurable: a consequentialist and a deontologist reasoning over the same vocabulary can disagree deductively rather than just terminologically.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must they share the same vocabulary?
five_whys_trace.a: Without shared vocabulary, 'compare two philosophies' is not expressible — you'd be comparing two unrelated ontologies with no common referents.
OWL 2 DL provides a decidable description logic with sound, complete reasoners — deductive comparison is then a formal query, not a vague approximation.

BFO provides an upper ontology with stable, well-studied categories (process, role, disposition, quality) that moral concepts naturally fall under — giving the TBox an external anchor that isn't itself the product of any one ethical theory.

five_whys_trace.q: Why ship the TBox before the framework modules?
five_whys_trace.a: The framework modules (consequentialism, deontology, virtue ethics) will each add axioms over this shared vocabulary. Without the vocabulary first, the framework modules have nothing to reference — dependencies must be built before their dependents.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should ousia-forge be a hard dependency (fail if absent) or soft (skip with note)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Soft for build-core (exit 0 with logged note if absent — enables cargo test on machines without ousia-forge); hard for check-core (exit non-zero if forge absent, because check-core's purpose IS validation).
